title: Remote ePHI Access — MFA Gap for VPN + O365 Clinical Mail

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="AzureActiveDirectory" Operation="UserLoggedIn" earliest=-24h
| eval mfa=if(match(ExtendedProperties,"MFA\s*completed|multi-factor"),1,0)
| lookup hipaa_ephi_users.csv user OUTPUT in_ephi_group
| where in_ephi_group="true" AND mfa=0
| stats count by user, ClientIP, ApplicationId
| sort - count
value: Surfaces successful VPN or Exchange Online logons without MFA claim for users in ePHI-privileged groups so remote access meets organizational authentication policy.
implementation: (1) Confirm `ExtendedProperties` field names for your tenant; (2) include Conditional Access exclusions report; (3) feed to IAM for forced enrollment.
visualization: Table (user, IP, count), Map (ClientIP), Single value (non-MFA logons).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
